bibliographic_data.title: Investigating Quantum Circuit Designs Using Neuro-Evolution
bibliographic_data.abstract: Designing effective quantum circuits remains a central challenge in quantum computing, as circuit structure strongly influences expressivity, trainability, and hardware feasibility. Current approaches, whether using manually designed circuit templates, fixed heuristics, or automated rules, face limitations in scalability, flexibility, and adaptability, often producing circuits that are poorly matched to the specific problem or quantum hardware. In this work, we propose the Evolutionary eXploration of Augmenting Quantum Circuits (EXAQC), an evolutionary approach to the automated design and training of parameterized quantum circuits (PQCs) which leverages and extends on strategies from neuroevolution and genetic programming. The proposed method jointly searches over gate types, qubit connectivity, parameterization, and circuit depth while respecting hardware and noise constraints. The method supports both Qiskit and Pennylane libraries, allowing the user to configure every aspect. This work highlights evolutionary search as a critical tool for advancing quantum machine learning and variational quantum algorithms, providing a principled pathway toward scalable, problem-aware, and hardware-efficient quantum circuit design. Preliminary results demonstrate that circuits evolved on classification tasks are able to achieve over 90% accuracy on most of the benchmark datasets with a limited computational budget, and are able to emulate target circuit quantum states with high fidelity scores.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper introduces EXAQC, an evolutionary approach for designing parameterized quantum circuits (PQCs) that jointly optimizes circuit structure and parameters for supervised learning tasks, using strategies from neuroevolution and genetic programming.
core_content.key_methodology: EXAQC leverages evolutionary algorithms to search over gate types, qubit connectivity, parameterization, and circuit depth while respecting hardware constraints. It supports both Qiskit and Pennylane libraries, allowing flexible configuration of the design process.
outcomes: Circuits evolved on classification tasks achieved over 90% accuracy on benchmark datasets with limited computational resources.
The method provides a scalable, problem-aware, and hardware-efficient pathway for quantum circuit design.
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this work, the authors propose EXAQC (Evolutionary eXploration of Augmenting Quantum Circuits), an evolutionary approach to designing parameterized quantum circuits (PQCs) that leverages strategies from neuroevolution and genetic programming. The method jointly searches over gate types, qubit connectivity, parameterization, and circuit depth while respecting hardware constraints. It supports both Qiskit and Pennylane libraries, allowing the user to configure every aspect of the design process. Preliminary results show that circuits evolved on classification tasks can achieve over 90% accuracy on benchmark datasets with limited computational resources. This work highlights evolutionary search as a critical tool for advancing quantum machine learning and variational quantum algorithms.
